items.briefing:
Australia has tightened rules around critical infrastructure security, meaning organisations in sectors like energy, health, and education face stricter risk management obligations — worth checking if your organisation falls under these requirements. Separately, phishing attacks are becoming more sophisticated, automatically adjusting their appearance based on what device or operating system you're using, making them harder to spot. A technique called "ClickFix" is also increasingly being used to trick people into running malicious commands by disguising them as routine prompts.

**Practical tip:** If a webpage or pop-up asks you to copy and paste a command into your computer, stop and contact your IT team immediately — this is a common sign of a ClickFix attack.

featured.title: Australia critical infrastructure rules tighten risk
featured.quote: Australia's critical infrastructure operators face stricter rules managing AI, legacy systems, and supplier risks.
